data.title: Banyan, Mandai open wildlife resort
data.description:
It is the first resort within a nature destination in a capital city.

Banyan Group and Mandai Wildlife Group recently opened the Mandai Rainforest Resort by Banyan Tree, which is said to be the first resort within a nature destination in a capital city.

Located in the Mandai Wildlife Reserve, the resort is Banyan Group’s 100th destination. It also marked the firm’s return to Singapore.

The resort guests can visit the Banyan Tree Spa and Gallery. The resort features sustainable design and supports biodiversity, a release said.

The opening of Mandai Rainforest Resort coincided with the Rainforest Festival that will run until 3 December. The festival visitors will be offered activities such as nature walks and yoga classes, amongst others.

Proceeds from the festival’s ticket sales will be donated by Mandai Wildlife Group and Banyan Gallery with dollar-to-dollar matching under the SG60 Matching Grants, a release said.

data.title: MM2 Asia receives $190,000 demand after lease termination
data.description:
The company seeks legal advice following notice from HPE.

MM2 Asia Ltd announced it has received a Termination and Demand Notice from HPE Facilities Services Sdn. Bhd. on 26 November 2025. The notice was issued to MM2 Screen Management Sdn. Bhd., with MM2 Asia acting as guarantor.

HPE terminated the business lease agreements with MSM and demanded payment of around $190,000 (RM604.7k), representing outstanding lease payments under the terminated contracts. The sum must be paid within seven days of the notice.

The Board of Directors said it is seeking legal advice in relation to the demand. The company stated it will make further announcements when there are material developments.

(1 RM = 0.31 SGD)

data.title: Singapore tops global talent index as governance and education strengthen
data.description:
Singapore also ranked first globally in formal education, regulatory landscape, and generalist adaptive skills.

Singapore has taken the top spot in the Global Talent Competitiveness Index 2025 for the first time, ahead of Switzerland, which moves to second place.

The report credits Singapore’s rise to effective governance, high living standards, a rapidly evolving education system, and stronger talent retention, with its retention rank improving by seven places to 31st.

Singapore also ranked first globally in formal education, regulatory landscape, and generalist adaptive skills.

The report’s top 10 rankings are: Singapore (1st), Switzerland (2nd), Denmark (3rd), Finland (4th), Sweden (5th), the Netherlands (6th), Norway (7th), Luxembourg (8th), the United States (9th), and Australia (10th).

Several major economies lost ground. The United States fell from 3rd in 2023 to 9th, weighed down by weaker scores in generalist adaptive skills and the attract and enable pillars, though it remains strong in growing talent and vocational and technical skills.

China dropped to 53rd from 40th, citing a weaker enabling environment, though the report notes some results are affected by data availability. India inched up to 100th, whilst Brazil (68th) and Mexico (70th) also improved.

The study highlighted countries that “punch above their weight” in talent efficiency, the ability to convert inputs into strong talent outcomes.

This group includes Israel, Singapore, South Korea, and several lower-middle-income countries such as Tajikistan, Kenya, Uzbekistan, Sri Lanka, Myanmar, Pakistan, and Bangladesh, showing that competitiveness is not solely determined by national wealth.

data.title: UOB and Stellar Lifestyle sign MoU as tenant liquidity support expands
data.description:
UOB will also look at supply-chain financing solutions for SMRT’s vendors.

UOB and SMRT’s Stellar Lifestyle have signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at easing liquidity pressures for MRT station tenants and expanding joint retail, digital, and mobility initiatives across the network.

Under the agreement, new and re-contracting tenants can replace cash rental deposits with UOB banker’s guarantees, potentially freeing up as much as three months of rent for working capital.

Tenants will also gain access to UOB BizSmart, a suite of digital tools covering accounting, HR and payroll, point-of-sale systems, and digital marketing, alongside complimentary or discounted services and guidance from UOB digital advisers.

On the retail front, promotions will feature on Stellar Lifestyle’s WINK+ app, and both parties will collaborate under the Stellarate Innovation Programme to trial new retail and advertising technologies and run joint marketing activities across MRT stations.

The partnership extends into mobility services through STRIDES, with plans to explore limousine services for selected UOB cardholders, partnerships across taxi, limousine, and cross-border (Singapore–Malaysia) transport, and referrals of car buyers to UOB for preferential auto loans.

UOB will also look at supply-chain financing solutions for SMRT’s vendors and offer sustainability-linked financing frameworks and advisory services. Its FDI Advisory unit will support the regional expansion of SMRT partners.

The partnership seeks to strengthen MRT stations as retail and services hubs by improving tenant liquidity, digitising SME operations, expanding mobility add-ons, and extending financing and ESG support across SMRT’s ecosystem.

data.title: Singapore’s Business Receipts Index rises 5.3% as services expand
data.description:
The main laggards were transportation and storage, down 7.5% due to weaker earnings.

Singapore’s Business Receipts Index for services industries excluding trade, retail, accommodation, and food services rose 5.3% YoY in the third quarter of 2025, according to SingStat.

On a non-seasonally adjusted basis, receipts were 0.7% higher QoQ. The index stood at about 176.7 in Q3, up from 175.5 in Q2 and slightly above 176.4 in Q4 2024.

Growth was broad-based but led by recreation and personal services, which increased 17.7% YoY on the back of stronger gaming activity.

Information and communications rose 12.0%, supported by gains in games publishing, data analytics and hosting, and search engine services. Professional services grew 10.4%, followed by finance and insurance, up by 6.3%; real estate, up by 5.0%; health and social services, up by 4.6%; and education, up by 4.4%.

The main laggards were transportation and storage, down 7.5% due to weaker earnings among shipping lines and freight arrangers, and administrative and support services, which fell 1.6%.

Quarter-on-quarter, professional services rose 3.4%, health and social services 3.0%, Education 2.9%, transportation and storage 2.4%, finance and insurance 1.9%, and real estate 1.0%.

Declines were recorded in administrative and support services, down by 3.7%; information and communications, down by 3.2%; and recreation and personal services, down by 0.8%.

data.title: Colliers completes $56.65m sale and leaseback of Framework Building
data.description:
The building is located near Paya Lebar and features a flexible layout.

Colliers Industrial Services completed a sale and leaseback transaction for the Framework Building with AIMS APAC REIT acquiring the property for $56.65m.

Located at 2 Aljunied Avenue 1 near the Paya Lebar commercial hub, the Framework Building has a gross floor area (GFA) of approximately 18,662 square metres, Colliers said.

Colliers will lease back 70% of the GFA for five years to ensure operational continuity and unlock capital, it added.

The property’s flexible layouts and high-power capacity make it suitable for advanced sectors such as healthcare, life sciences, and precision manufacturing, said Edmund Lee, executive director, industrial services, Colliers Singapore.

The transaction aligned with the market momentum highlighted in Colliers’ Q3 2025 Industrial & Logistics Insights, which showed that amidst tightening vacancy rates and rising prime logistics rents, demand continues to shift toward high-specification assets.

The deal was brokered by the firm’s Edmund Lee and Raphael Lee.

data.title: Condo resale prices hit record high as volume eases
data.description:
Median capital gains rose to $401,000 with varied district outcomes.

Condo resale prices reached new highs in October 2025 even as sales volume eased, according to a report from 99-SRX. The overall resale price index increased 1% month on month (MoM) and 5.3% year on year (YoY), with gains recorded across all regions.

CCR resale prices rose 0.8% in October, RCR climbed 2.3%, and OCR inched up 0.2%. On an annual basis, CCR, RCR and OCR posted increases of 3.6%, 6.2% and 5% respectively.

An estimated 1,069 units changed hands in October, down 4.5% from September’s 1,119 units and 4.7% below levels a year earlier. Volumes were also 1.6% under the five-year average. OCR made up 47.7% of transactions, followed by RCR at 29.4% and CCR at 22.8%. Sub-sale activity rose to 6.4% of transactions from 5.1% in September.

The month’s highest resale transaction was $14.5m for a unit at Nassim Lodge in CCR. RCR’s top deal was $5.15m at Aalto, whilst OCR’s highest recorded $3.58m at Citylife@Tampines.

Median capital gains rose to $401,000, up $42,000 from September. District 10 posted the highest median gain at $754,000, whilst District 1 recorded a median loss of $54,000. Median unlevered returns reached 30.4%, led by District 20 at 48.2%, whilst District 1 registered -2.6%.

The figures reflect a market where overall prices continue to advance, whilst district-level gains remain uneven.

data.title: Singapore shows most trust in fintech in Southeast Asia: report
data.description:
Over 90% in the city-state placed high trust on technology’s transparency, security, and regulation.

Consumers in Singapore showed the most trust in financial technology (fintech) services in Southeast Asia as 91.8% reported high confidence in the technology, UraFinancial said.

Singapore showed the highest trust level, with 69.1% saying they are somewhat trusting, whilst 22.1% report high trust. The remaining 8.8% are neutral. Singapore is followed by the Philippines with 88.1%.

The main drivers of trust in the city-state are transparency of fees and terms (60.3%), security of financial data (58.8%), brand reputation (57.4%), regulatory oversight (55.4%), and ease of use (54.4%), it added.

Meanwhile, 35.3% of respondents tend to trust fintech services as recommended by family and friends, whilst only 28% rely on their own past experience.

Eight in ten respondents (84.3%) in the four Southeast Asian countries that also included Vietnam and Indonesia said they either highly trust (29.3%) or somewhat trust (55%) fintech platforms.

Only 15.0% are neutral and fewer than 1% report low or no trust, UraFinancial said.

In Vietnam and Indonesia, 37.2% and 29.4%, respectively, highly trust fintech. 

In the Philippines, brand reputation is influential as 73.8% of respondents said it affects their trust, the group observed.

Security and fee transparency are close behind (both 69.1%), followed by user experience (66.7%), it added.

data.title: Lendlease REIT completes PLQ Mall deal, deploys $234.5m placement funds
data.description:
About $45.5m was also earmarked for fees and debt reduction.

Lendlease Global Commercial REIT has completed the acquisition of a 70% stake in PLQ Mall via units in PLQM Trust and 70% of the trustee‑manager of PLQM Trust, according to a filing.

The transaction was financed through a private placement launched on 5 November 2025, which raised gross proceeds of approximately $280.0m. Of this, $234.5m (83.7%) has been applied to fund the consideration for the sale units and shares, slightly above the allocated amount disclosed earlier.

The remaining $45.5m (16.3%) will be used to cover professional fees and expenses related to the acquisition and placement, and to pare down existing debt.

The manager said the reallocation reduced proceeds originally earmarked for debt repayment by $0.2m.

data.title: Health ministry suspends Cordlife’s blood banking license
data.description:
Cordlife is suspended from collecting, testing, processing, or storing new cord blood units.

The Ministry of Health (MoH) has suspended Cordlife Group Limited from collecting, testing, processing or storing new cord blood units (CBUs) with effect from 26 November 2025.

This is after Cordlife responded to an intent to suspend its license from MoH.

After considering Cordlife’s response, the firm had not adequately addressed the concerns raised during MoH’s audit in July 2025 to the cord blood banking service, the MoH said in a statement.

It failed to address that its services are done in a safe and ethically appropriate manner, and Cordlife requires time to address the outstanding issues, it added.

Cordlife is suspended from collecting, testing, processing and/or storing new cord blood, conditions that will remain even if Cordlife renews its licence in January 2026 and until it meets the regulatory requirements for cord blood banking services, the MoH said.

This is to ensure that the firm continues to meet its obligations under the Healthcare Services Act and the safety of CBUs already stored with Cordlife by existing clients.

The MoH also maintains the earlier directions to Cordlife, including to replace its current clinical governance officer and review all laboratory records of the approximately 160 CBUs collected since January 2025, it added.
